learnings: `java.io.PushbackReader` plus char-by-char reads is a practical way in Clojure CLI to distinguish newline-terminated input from partial EOF input for terminal UX rules.
Be careful not to shadow current board state when destructuring move results; invalid-move redraws must use the pre-move board while valid-move resolution uses the updated board.
result_payload.summary: Implemented the full terminal human-vs-human tic-tac-toe game behind `make run`, including welcome-to-board flow, numbered/colorized board rendering, win/draw detection, in-memory session scoreboard, strict move parsing, play-again loop, ANSI clear/redraw transitions, and graceful EOF handling. Added focused automated tests, ran `make check`, manually QA’d key gameplay/EOF scenarios, and passed the Ralph validator.
